name: Natural Gas / LNG
summary: Day 103 / 2026-06-10 (Wednesday; THREE-DAY window June 7 cutoff -> early June 10, June 8/9 runs empty; deltas vs June 7). **THE DRIVER SPLIT IS THE WHOLE STORY — HENRY HUB AND EUROPEAN TTF SHARE A NUMBER BUT NOT A CAUSE; DO NOT ENCODE HENRY HUB STRENGTH AS CRISIS.** Henry Hub $3.22 (+2.6% 1D, +10.7% 1M) is US SUMMER-HEAT DEMAND (above-normal temps forecast through June 24) DESPITE ample storage (~5% above 5-yr avg) and softer LNG-export flows (seasonal maintenance Golden Pass/Freeport) — ~0% crisis, a DOMESTIC WEATHER story that does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal. European TTF ~EUR51/MWh (+~5% 1D on Jun 9, +~11% 1M) is the CRISIS-LINKED gauge: ~70% crisis on Qatar's ~17% liquefaction (Ras Laffan) offline + Iran-Israel escalation, still sub-EUR70. The two legs share elevated 1M prints but are causally orthogonal — bundling Henry Hub's +10.7% MoM into the energy-crisis read would be a textbook attribution error. Cheniere (LNG) $239.40 (+1.2%) is the US-LNG bid ON the TTF strength, not on Henry Hub weather. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 10)**: TTF EUR100 / TTF EUR70-sustained triggers STAY watching (~EUR51, not crossed). FIELD CLARIFICATION (markets Section E), not a new node: the `current` object now distinguishes HH (domestic/weather, ~0% crisis) from TTF (Qatar/Hormuz, ~70% crisis). PRIOR — Day 97 (Thursday June 4; covers 3 June afternoon -> 4 June US day / 5 June IST). **HENRY HUB +22.7% MoM IS WEATHER, NOT WAR — KEEP IT CLEANLY SEPARATE; CHENIERE +2.4% ON THE HEAT-SURGE LNG BID; THE CRISIS-LINKED GAUGE (TTF) IS FLAT.** US Henry Hub $3.35 (+0.4% 1D, +22.7% MoM) on an unprecedented East-Coast heatwave (above-normal temps forecast through 18 Jun) -> cooling/gas-fired power demand, a sub-avg 95 Bcf EIA storage build (trailing year-ago + 5-yr avg), and a Lower-48 production dip to 109.0 bcfd — a US DOMESTIC weather/supply story with ZERO crisis content (markets C7); do NOT let the +22.7% MoM contaminate the energy-crisis read, and it does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal. Partial offset: LNG feedgas fell to 16.3 bcfd on seasonal terminal maintenance. It does partly explain Cheniere +2.4% ($241.07) — a US gas/LNG bid on the heat-surge + the structural LNG-substitution premium. The crisis-linked gas gauge is European TTF (EUR48.76/MWh, -0.2% 1D, +33% YoY, storage only 38% full ahead of winter) — flat today, structural premium intact (Qatar Ras Laffan ~17% force majeure persists, a 3-5yr recovery NOT deal-contingent). The natural-gas-lng->brent-crude correlation edge stays DOWN-WEIGHTED (3.5): the live Henry Hub driver is domestic weather, not a Brent co-move. PRIOR — Day 95 (Monday June 2; covers 30 May afternoon -> 2 June morning). **THE TWO STORIES SPLIT AGAIN: CHENIERE +5.0% (CRISIS RE-PRICING + THAILAND CONTRACT) WHILE EUROPEAN TTF FELL -2.85% (THE NON-CONFIRMATION ON THE PHYSICAL GAS CURVE). On the re-escalation (Iran's suspension + Hormuz 'complete closure' threat), Cheniere bounced +5.0% to $239.20 — ~70% crisis (Hormuz threat + Ras Laffan legacy -> US Gulf Coast LNG pivot) / ~30% NON-crisis (Thailand expanded its Cheniere offtake 1.0->1.3M t/yr; do NOT 100%-crisis-code). BUT European TTF gas FELL -2.85% to EUR47.69/MWh (still +32% YoY) — on a 'complete Hormuz closure' threat day, the leg that 'should' spike (Qatar LNG transits Hormuz) instead eased: the closure is a THREAT not an act, the operational chokehold is unchanged, and Europe is trading its own fundamentals (38% storage fill, summer injection, Norway supply). This confirms the day's pattern — threats re-priced FUTURES (Brent paper) but did NOT move the PHYSICAL European gas curve. US Henry Hub $3.15 (+24% MoM) remains a NON-crisis domestic story (storage/production/LNG-export ramp). GRAPH NOTE: cheniere/natural-gas-lng -> thailand is a NEW non-crisis demand edge (markets E2) — noted for attribution hygiene so future Cheniere moves aren't 100%-crisis-coded; not yet a formal node. Watch TTF only if the closure goes operational. PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30; Friday 29 May close). **TWO DIVERGENT STORIES: THE QATAR-DISRUPTION LNG PREMIUM IS CRACKING (CHENIERE -17% MoM) WHILE US HENRY HUB RIPS +24% MoM ON NON-CRISIS DOMESTIC SUPPLY/DEMAND. Cheniere -17.2% MoM (-2.1% 1D) as deal-anticipation de-prices the Hormuz/LNG tail; European TTF gas eased to EUR45.96/MWh (-2.2% 1D) but holds a ~+30% YoY floor (Qatar Ras Laffan ~17% force majeure persists). Separately and NON-crisis: US Henry Hub +24.3% MoM to $3.29 on storage injections below forecast (92 bcf vs 95-96 exp) + an LNG-export ramp (Golden Pass Train 1, Corpus Christi Stage 3 Train 5) + easing production — a domestic supply/demand story, NOT the war. The Qatar-premium crack is on the FAST legs (equity, TTF); the SLOW legs (insurance/freight) and helium remain elevated/unverified.** Day 90 (Thursday May 28). **HENRY HUB $3.27 (+5.8% 1D, +27.8% MoM, +10.5% 3M) — A FUNDAMENTAL, DOMESTIC-US MOVE (~90% NON-CRISIS), DISTINCT FROM THE TTF / QATAR-LNG CRISIS CHANNEL. DO NOT bundle this into the 'war drives energy' narrative, and it does NOT reverse on an Iran deal.** The drivers are all domestic US fundamentals tightening the near-term balance: (1) EIA weekly storage build of 92 bcf (week ended May 22) came in BELOW the 95-96 bcf forecast (a bullish surprise); (2) US Lower-48 production eased to 109.4 bcfd (from 109.8 in April); (3) flows to LNG export plants declined on seasonal maintenance. Still -7% YoY — a range move, not a historic extreme. **TWO DISTINCT CHANNELS, KEPT SEPARATE: (A) the DOMESTIC Henry Hub story above (storage/production/maintenance — does NOT reverse on a deal); (B) the CRISIS channel via European TTF + Qatar Ras Laffan**, where TTF holds its structural premium (~+30% YoY floor) because Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) force majeure is a 3-5yr recovery, NOT deal-contingent, and US LNG terminals run near-max because Hormuz/Ras Laffan took ~1/5 of global LNG offline. The natural-gas-lng→brent-crude correlation edge stays DOWN-WEIGHTED (3.5): the live Henry Hub driver is domestic fundamentals, not a Brent co-move. PRIOR (May 27): Day 89 (Wednesday May 27 — ~48h since the May 25 brief). **HENRY HUB DECOUPLED FROM THE CRISIS-OIL COMPLEX — $3.15, +8.8% 1D / +23.5% MoM TO ITS HIGHEST SINCE MARCH, ON A US EAST COAST HEAT WAVE + A DROP IN US PRODUCTION. THIS IS WEATHER (~85% NON-CRISIS), NOT A CRISIS SIGNAL (C4).** Henry Hub fell when oil fell earlier in the cycle; now it RISES on US weather while Brent DE-prices on the deal — the correlation BROKE this cycle. Do NOT read the +8.8% nat-gas spike as a crisis signal. **The crisis-relevant gas benchmark is European TTF (€46.22/MWh, -3.0% 1D, +30% YoY floor)**, which eased on deal hopes but holds its structural premium. The structural LNG underlay (~15%) holds: US LNG export terminals at near-maximum utilization because the Hormuz/Ras Laffan disruption took ~1/5 of global LNG flows offline — so the LEVEL carries a crisis premium, but the +8.8% 1D move is domestic weather/production. Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) force majeure is a 3-5yr recovery, NOT deal-contingent. **GRAPH NOTE (markets E2): the natural-gas-lng→brent-crude correlation edge is DELIBERATELY DOWN-WEIGHTED this cycle (9.0 → 3.5) — the live Henry Hub driver is domestic weather/production, NOT a Brent co-move; the only crisis transmission runs through the structural LNG-export-utilization channel to strait-of-hormuz, not a direct flat-price correlation.** Cheniere -10.3% MoM = the LNG-equity crisis trade unwinding even as physical terminals run near-max. PRIOR (May 25): **HENRY HUB $3.02 FROZEN (Friday May 22 close — NYMEX did not settle; do NOT read as a Monday move). What traded LIVE: European TTF Gas €46.63/MWh, -4.2% 1D — the deal-relief leg (US-Iran deal progress + 3 LNG vessels crossed Hormuz), co-moving with Brent sub-$100, urea -7.4%, India +1.3%. BUT the structural LNG floor holds: TTF +25% YoY, +4.4% MoM. Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) force majeure is a 3-5yr recovery, NOT contingent on the Iran deal — so the gas/LNG premium is durable. C4 paper-physical non-confirmation: paper prices the deal, physical prices ZERO operational change; even on a clean signed deal the physical gas/LNG cost stack bleeds out over 6-12 weeks, not in a session — 3 LNG vessels crossing Hormuz is a thin first trickle, not a reopening. Cheniere $240.85 FROZEN (Friday) — LNG-basis structural bid intact, US LNG fungibility into European-emergency supply structurally bid. Reversibility: paper component reverses on Pakistan-track failure or Israeli kinetic action; physical Qatar recovery is years out regardless of diplomatic resolution.** PRIOR (May 23): **HENRY HUB NATURAL GAS $2.91 MAY 22 CLOSE (-3.7% 1D, +11.2% MoM, -2.6% 3M) — FRIDAY'S DROP = EIA STORAGE REPORT (92 Bcf INJECTION IN-LINE, BUILDS SURPLUS); 1M PREMIUM = LNG-BASIS STORY.** Per markets dossier C8: EIA weekly storage report Thursday showed 92 Bcf injection in line with 5Y-average, holding the +140 Bcf surplus to seasonal norm for the third consecutive week (Natural Gas Intelligence) → Friday session priced through storage-comfortable read → -3.7% sell-off. Henry Hub close $2.91 below the 2026 expected average of $3.50. **The +11.2% 1M is the LNG-basis story**: Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure (March 2 with 17% liquefaction loss persistent) + tightness in European LNG supply spread → US LNG export demand bid → 1M Henry Hub differential vs storage-implied price. **TTF Gas €48.69/MWh May 22 close** (-1.46% 1D, +2.1% MoM, +30%+ vs ~€31 baseline) — flat-to-easing; Qatar Ras Laffan FM 17% liquefaction persists but Norway maintenance ease holding. **Cheniere Energy (LNG) $240.85** (+0.2% 1D, -6.1% 1M, +8.1% 3M) — LNG-basis structural bid intact. **Friday's -3.7% is a domestic storage signal; the +11.2% 1M is a crisis-linked LNG-basis signal. Both correct interpretations at their respective timeframes.** Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. PRIOR (May 19): **Henry Hub $3.04/MMBtu (+2.8% 1D, +13.8% 1M, +1.1% 3M)** — Summer cooling + Qatar LNG complex still 20% offline; sympathy with TTF. **B4 European TTF Gas €50.16/MWh — flat versus Friday €50.85 close** (within daily noise); past month +18.26%; +42.75% YoY; ~20% of global LNG supply disrupted since war began. Friday's +6.7% one-day move NOT extending Monday — gas complex treating maximal-pressure weekend tape as already priced. Cheniere Energy (LNG) $241.84 +0.3% 1D (-7.7% 1M, +10.7% 3M); Cheniere CCO 'astounded prices not higher' framing carries forward. Heating Oil $3.98 -1.9% 1D / +17.1% 1M / +58.0% 3M (refiner slate consolidating after +58% 3M distillate ramp). RBOB Gasoline $3.59 -3.1% 1D (counter-trend after +82% 3M). **Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery — physical Qatar LNG outage continues to provide structural floor.** Hormuz functionally throttled at ~30 ships/day per Aramco (partially explained by Iran-permitted Chinese selective transit). **Brent $111.47 +2.0% Monday (StanChart $130-145 path activates — 2-session sustain rule met)** — natural-gas-LNG bid carries Brent's risk premium via energy complex linkage. **PRIOR (May 16): Henry Hub $2.96/MMBtu (+2.3% 1D, +11.8% 1M, -2.3% 3M) — LNG-global tightness pull (TTF €49.86) + Hormuz LNG dislocation** (~70% crisis). **B4 TTF €49.86/MWh +4.63% 1D, third consecutive daily increase and second-consecutive >1% move; highest since April** — driver chain ongoing Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure on ~17% of liquefaction capacity + Hormuz tensions + Europe pre-winter storage rebuild. **Cheniere (LNG) $241.84 +0.3% 1D / -7.7% 1M / +10.7% 3M** — long-term LNG hedged at pre-crisis prices remains the headwind; equity-multiple compression on crisis-unwind expectation continues even as physical complex tightens. Heating Oil $4.05 +3.8% 1D / +69.6% 3M (oil pass-through, distillate-tight slate persists). RBOB Gasoline $3.70 +2.7% 1D / +17.0% 1M (refiner slate shift toward distillates + Brent extension + summer-driving prep). **Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery — physical Qatar LNG outage continues to provide structural floor.** Hormuz functionally throttled at ~30 ships/day per Aramco (partially explained by Iran-permitted Chinese selective transit). ECB Lagarde 'new energy shock' May 6 framing carries forward into the regime read. PRIOR (May 14): **Henry Hub $2.89/MMBtu (+0.8% 1D, +10.6% 1M, -4.8% 3M)** — Hormuz-LNG bid spillover via TTF arbitrage; Cheniere supply pricing pulling US gas higher (~80% crisis). **TTF €46.63/MWh May 13 (-0.11% 1D, basically flat at +47% from pre-war €31.20)** — sustained-elevated mode, neither alarm nor relief (B4). **C3 Cheniere (LNG) $239.38 (-2.0% 1D, -7.6% 1M, +9.6% 3M)** = $3.5bn Q1 hedging derivatives loss from Iran-war price blowout + LNG infrastructure trade-unwind on diminishing Hormuz-tail-risk + Trump-Xi opening = US LNG-to-China future demand normalization (positive long-term but reduces crisis-premium short-term). **The Q1 hedging loss is a one-time print but the equity-multiple compression is the crisis-unwind signal.** Hormuz functionally throttled — Aramco CEO Nasser line carries forward (2-5 ships/day, 100M bbl/week lost). Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. **B4 TTF €46.63 + Container freight FBX13 stable = confirms quiet European-energy-channel.** PRIOR (May 13): TTF €46.23/MWh May 11 (+4.74% 1D, +5% from May 8 €44 baseline, +31.57% YoY) — stepped up on Trump rejection + Aramco 2-5 ships/day Hormuz deterioration. **Cheniere LNG $240.70 May 12 (+0.5% 1D, -9.1% 1M, +10.8% 3M) — Q1 2026 $3.5B GAAP loss on derivative MtM** still in price; CCO Anatol Feygin (May 7): 'astounded that global gas prices have not risen higher with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupting 100 cargoes a month.' US LNG players are SHORT the war via hedge books. **Hormuz functionally throttled — Aramco CEO Nasser May 11: 2-5 ships/day (DETERIORATED from May 10 ~6/day), 100M bbl/week lost, 880M cumulative, market won't normalize until 2027 if disruption persists past mid-June.** Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. **NEW VARIABLE:** WSJ May 11 revealed UAE secretly struck Iran's Lavan Island oil refinery April 8 (55,000 bpd offline months) — affects oil specifically, not LNG, but reframes Gulf-state covert military participation. Helium node update: Korean fab buffer ~4 weeks remaining to mid-June exhaustion. **Reversibility:** TTF/HH partial decoupling continues; Cheniere -9.1% 1M reflects markets pricing-through peace optionality despite operational tightness. Qatar Ras Laffan reopening is in any MOU framework but physical recovery years out regardless of diplomatic resolution.

recent_signals.headline: LNG complex easing on the framework — TTF -5% to EUR44, Cheniere -2.5%; the prospective Hormuz reopening (~1/5 of global LNG transit) deflates the scarcity premium. Physical normalization still lags (strait closed Day 107; LNG shipments had been cut ~99%). REPORTED/CONFIRMED.

Henry Hub +1.7% on US weather/storage (~zero crisis). The crisis-linked gauge is European TTF (~EUR48-49, easing on the deal), not Henry Hub. A signed deal reopening Hormuz would relieve the ~19%-of-global-LNG transit risk the strait carries.

Henry Hub -3.0% on US weather/storage -- ~zero crisis content (do NOT encode as crisis). The crisis-linked gas gauge is European TTF (EUR50.26, +0.5%, +7.65% 1M), NOT Henry Hub. Iran's formal Hormuz closure (the strait carries ~a fifth of global LNG) drew only a half-percent TTF move -- storage-refill framing, not panic.

European TTF ~EUR51/MWh (+~5% 1D Jun 9, +~11% 1M) is the CRISIS gauge — ~70% crisis on Qatar's ~17% liquefaction (Ras Laffan) offline + Iran-Israel escalation, still sub-EUR70. The two legs share elevated 1M prints but are causally orthogonal; bundling Henry Hub's +10.7% MoM into the energy-crisis read is a textbook attribution error. Cheniere (LNG) $239.40 (+1.2%) is the US-LNG bid ON the TTF strength, not on Henry Hub weather. recent_signals.headline: **HENRY HUB $3.27 (+5.8% 1D, +27.8% MoM, +10.5% 3M) — A FUNDAMENTAL, DOMESTIC-US MOVE (~90% NON-CRISIS), DISTINCT FROM THE TTF / QATAR-LNG CRISIS CHANNEL. (2) **Range move, not a historic extreme**: Henry Hub is still -7% YoY. (3) **DO NOT bundle this into the 'war drives energy' narrative**: this is a separate, DOMESTIC US story, distinct from the European TTF / Qatar-LNG crisis channel — and it does NOT reverse on an Iran deal. (4) **Two channels kept separate**: (A) the domestic Henry Hub story above; (B) the CRISIS channel via European TTF + Qatar Ras Laffan, where TTF holds its ~+30% YoY structural premium because Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) force majeure is a 3-5yr recovery, NOT deal-contingent, and US LNG terminals run near-max because Hormuz/Ras Laffan took ~1/5 of global LNG offline. (5) **Edge**: the natural-gas-lng→brent-crude correlation edge stays DOWN-WEIGHTED (3.5) — the live Henry Hub driver is domestic fundamentals, not a Brent co-move.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-05-28 C6 (Henry Hub $3.27 +5.8% 1D / +27.8% MoM / +10.5% 3M; EIA storage build 92 bcf BELOW 95-96 bcf forecast; production eased to 109.4 bcfd; LNG-export flows down on seasonal maintenance; ~90% NON-crisis domestic; does NOT reverse on a deal; still -7% YoY)

recent_signals.headline: **HENRY HUB DECOUPLED FROM THE CRISIS-OIL COMPLEX — $3.15, +8.8% 1D / +23.5% MoM TO ITS HIGHEST SINCE MARCH ON A US EAST COAST HEAT WAVE + A DROP IN US PRODUCTION. THIS IS WEATHER (~85% NON-CRISIS), NOT A CRISIS SIGNAL (C4).** (1) **The driver (~85% NON-crisis)**: an unprecedented US East Coast / Lower-48 heat wave drove early-summer cooling demand + a drop in US production pushed prompt-month Henry Hub to its highest since March (Natural Gas Intelligence). (2) **The decoupling is the intelligence**: Henry Hub FELL when oil fell earlier in the cycle; now it RISES on US weather while Brent DE-prices ~-14% MoM on the deal. The Henry-Hub↔Brent correlation BROKE this cycle. Do NOT read the +8.8% spike as a crisis signal. (3) **The crisis-relevant gas benchmark is European TTF (€46.22/MWh, -3.0% 1D, +30% YoY)**, which eased on deal hopes but holds its structural premium above the ~€31 pre-war baseline. (4) **Structural LNG underlay (~15%)**: US LNG export terminals at near-maximum utilization because the Hormuz/Ras Laffan disruption took ~1/5 of global LNG flows offline — the LEVEL of US-international spreads carries a crisis premium, but the +8.8% 1D spike is domestic weather/production. Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) force majeure is a 3-5yr recovery, NOT deal-contingent. (5) **EDGE ACTION (markets E2)**: the natural-gas-lng→brent-crude correlation edge is DELIBERATELY DOWN-WEIGHTED 9.0 → 3.5 — the live Henry Hub driver is weather/production, not a Brent co-move; the only crisis transmission runs through the structural LNG-export-utilization channel to strait-of-hormuz. (6) **Cheniere -10.3% MoM** (LNG-equity crisis trade unwinding despite tight physical; tracked on the cheniere-energy node).

recent_signals.headline: **HENRY HUB $3.02 FROZEN (US MEMORIAL DAY — NYMEX DID NOT SETTLE); EUROPEAN TTF €46.63/MWh -4.2% LIVE ON DEAL-RELIEF + 3 LNG VESSELS CROSSED HORMUZ. STRUCTURAL LNG FLOOR HOLDS (TTF +25% YoY).** (1) **Data status**: Monday May 25 is US Memorial Day + UK Spring Bank Holiday — Henry Hub ($3.02) and Cheniere ($240.85) carry FROZEN Friday closes; do NOT read them as Monday moves. European TTF traded LIVE at €46.63, -4.2%. (2) **The LIVE move is the deal-relief leg**: US-Iran deal progress + 3 LNG vessels crossed Hormuz (a thin first trickle) re-priced the European gas tail premium DOWN, co-moving with Brent (sub-$100), urea (-7.4%), India equities (+1.3%). (3) **But the structural floor holds**: TTF +4.4% MoM, +25% YoY vs ~€31 pre-war. Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) force majeure is a 3-5yr recovery, NOT contingent on Iran deal architecture. (4) **C4 paper-physical non-confirmation**: paper prices the deal as near-done; physical (gas/LNG, insurance, shipping) prices ZERO operational change. Even on a clean signed deal the physical gas/LNG cost stack bleeds out over 6-12 weeks — 3 LNG vessels crossing Hormuz is not a reopening. (5) **Cheniere structural bid intact** (FROZEN $240.85) — US LNG fungibility into European-emergency supply remains structurally bid; Hormuz reopening is the bearish reversal but reopen-timeline is 2H26 (Baker Hughes). **Reversibility**: paper component reverses on Pakistan-track failure or Israeli kinetic action; physical Qatar recovery is years out regardless of diplomatic resolution.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-05-25 STRUCTURAL NOTE (Henry Hub $3.02 + Cheniere FROZEN; US Memorial Day, NYMEX did not settle)

recent_signals.headline: **CHENIERE +2.4% — EU STORAGE 141 CARGOES BELOW 5-YEAR LOW + RAISED 2026 EBITDA GUIDE TO $7.25-7.75B; HORMUZ LNG BASIS STRUCTURAL.** European TTF €50.17/MWh holds elevated; Hormuz-LNG-dislocation re-priced US LNG basis structurally. **US LNG fungibility into European-emergency-supply is structurally bid; Hormuz reopening is bearish for this trade, but reopen-timeline says 2H26.** Henry Hub $3.02 (-0.3% 1D); +12.1% 1M (LNG export pull + Cheniere demand; partially crisis-driven via European basis). **Cheniere Energy as potential new node**: appears in 4+ independent sources as transmission mechanism between European TTF ↔ Hormuz LNG dislocation ↔ US LNG export.
recent_signals.verification: Cheniere +2.4% = CONFIRMED operational; $7.25-7.75B EBITDA guide = CONFIRMED; EU storage 141 cargoes below 5y low = REPORTED (Cheniere management); Henry Hub flat = CONFIRMED.

recent_signals.headline: SUNDAY HOLIDAY — TTF ~€44/MWh weekly (-25%+ from March €60+ peak, +40%+ vs Feb ~€31 baseline); Cheniere $240.11 May 8 close (-2.7% 1D, -9.7% 1M, +11.3% 3M); Q1 $3.5B GAAP loss on derivative MtM (long-term hedges struck pre-war at lower TTF/HH spread became deep underwater after Qatar force majeure spiked TTF €60+ in March). Cheniere CCO Anatol Feygin: 'astounded that global gas prices have not risen higher with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupting 100 cargoes a month.' 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance RAISED to $7.25-7.75B (from $6.75-7.25B). HEADLINE GAAP LOSS IS NON-CASH; CASH EARNINGS POWER INCREASED. **Sunday May 10 bulk-carrier-near-Doha strike is operationally adjacent to Ras Laffan — Qatar's main LNG/helium export complex.** If strike location was deliberate, signals graduated escalation against Qatari LNG export infrastructure rather than against US-blockade-facilitating shipping. Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. Cheniere is cleanest US-LNG-exporter beneficiary of any extended Qatar disruption.
